name: Elegant Stuffed Pumpkin
recipeIngredient: 2 cups crumbled dry whole wheat bread (or part cornbread)
½ to 1 cup chopped celery and leaves
1 cup roasted chestnuts or a handful of cashew nuts, halved
Herbs: sage, savory, marjoram, oregano, and paprika to taste (about ¼ teaspoon each)
¼–½ cup melted butter or safflower oil
Soy sauce or salt to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off the top of the pumpkin to make a lid. Remove the seeds and scrape out any stringy pulp.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine dry ingredients (whole wheat bread crumbs, cornmeal) and mix well with hands.
3. Add vegetable stock and butter; mix thoroughly. Season with soy sauce and salt if desired. The stuffing should be moist but not soggy.
4. Stuff the pumpkin loosely with the mixture. Replace the lid and place on an oiled cookie sheet.
5. Bake at 325°F for 1½ hours or more until a fork easily pierces the pumpkin.
6. Transfer to a casing dish and serve at the table, scooping out some of the tender pumpkin flesh with each serving of stuffing. If the pumpkin is organically grown, you may eat the skin as well. (If there's leftover stuffing, place extra in an oiled casserole, cover, and bake for 1 hour.)
